title: Cotoneaster royleanus
taxonomy: Cotoneaster royleanus is classified within the family Rosaceae, which includes a diverse range of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. This family consists of approximately 5,000 species, making it one of the largest and most widespread plant families in the world. The genus Cotoneaster, to which Cotoneaster royleanus belongs, comprises around 40 species of shrubs and small trees native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. Within the genus Cotoneaster, the species are often differentiated by their fruit characteristics, growth habits, and geographical distributions.

intro: Cotoneaster royleanus, also known as Himalayan Cotoneaster, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its bright red berries and attractive white flowers. The health benefits of Cotoneaster royleanus include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues.
